entries.name: Aldwin — Count Edmund's sister-son & now the last of his blood; the boy-regent Shake saved & made; holds besieged Hollowford on Shake's promise of relief; the next target of the line-killers

Count Halfdan — deep-north-backed northern marcher lord (Skarrgaard / Cloven Strand); a relief target whose horse could make the muster an army; moves for his own gain (the North Coast succession); a marriage to Eadgyth is the coin he'd want
Count Edmund — STRICKEN by apoplexy, half-paralyzed & aphasic, out of command; Shake's sworn (stricken) lord, in besieged Hollowford
Wulf — Count Edmund's doomsman/reeve, six-fingered; never a flat 'no'; ally left holding the keep's law; GM-secret: divided loyalties (the law/Crown above Edmund)
The Crown delegation (the Regency) — a relief sponsor to court (would relieve Hollowford to deny it to Ranulf); envoy Aldhelm already has Shake's name in a ledger

Captain Soren — foreign, mottled-skinned sell-sword commander of Hollowford's ~150 mercenary huscarls (the keep's spine); concealed sin; coin-loyal — and SUSPECTED bought by the Bone-Sayers (the spine may break from within)
The Stonebay marauder-captain — escaped, loose in the March, now moving with the Bone-Sayers & asking after Shake; a hostile loose end ahead on the road
The Ash-Captain — low-born foreign (Ostmark) warlord besieging Hollowford (~2,500-3,000); risen from nothing, secretly craving legitimacy; his host coordinated with the line-killers (one campaign)

Eadgyth — Count Edmund's daughter, riding with Shake; GRIEF-EMBITTERED since her love Aldred died at Edenmoor — half-blames Shake, names herself the marriage-price; a strained wildcard, not a biddable chip
Sir Oswin — grizzled household knight, 30 yrs sworn to Edmund's blood; Shake's second & voucher on the relief-ride; the thanes trust his word

Sir Godric — young idealist landed knight (10 lances), sworn to Shake's cause; the muster's first stone & a true-believer's banner; local Greenways knowledge; the man who pulled the first thread on the enemy within (and Shake's unwitting conscience re: holding Marlford for himself)
Sir Brunwulf — weathered older Greenways knight who sought Shake out; brought lances + ~20 mounted archers + 2 more halls' word; the muster's solid backbone
Brother Eadwig — old infirmarer-scholar of Edenmoor, saved by Shake & riding at his side; learning, medicine, & a Codex churchman's blessing (counters the 'drift-tainted' slur)

Thane Beorhtric of Stanmere — the March's senior hold-rank thane (~40 horse + 300 fyrd), the relief's would-be backbone; WILL answer but bears a bitter grudge vs Edmund's enclosures — prickly, courtable

Saewulf — grizzled retired free-company captain, 'last survivor' of his old band; held Marlford's keep against Cray, now Shake's sworn right-hand & veteran lieutenant (a soldier's mind, hard and his own man); brings the citadel's steady holdouts

Coll Cray (FLED) — the pox-scarred profiteer; Shake broke his grip & took Marlford but Cray slipped a river bolt-hole with his strongbox; loose, vengeful, will sell what he knows of Shake/the host/the plan to the highest bidder (the web)
